risk_description: Distributed transaction inconsistency during financial workflows (Stripe/Wise) where internal ledger updates occur but external gateway calls fail (or vice-versa), leading to financial discrepancies.
root_cause: Lack of atomic transactions across distributed microservices and external third-party APIs.
mitigation_strategy: Implement Saga pattern for distributed transactions; Enforce idempotency keys on all payment gateway requests; Develop automated nightly reconciliation cron jobs.
contingency_plan: Manual administrative override interface (US-065) to force-correct ledger states based on gateway audit logs.
monitoring_trigger: Detection of 'orphan' transaction records in Pending state > 1 hour or webhook signature verification failures.

risk_description: Node.js Event Loop blocking during bulk CSV ingestion (10k+ rows) or large SOW PDF parsing, causing API unavailability for concurrent users.
root_cause: CPU-intensive parsing operations running on the main thread of Node.js microservices.
mitigation_strategy: Offload CSV parsing and PDF extraction to dedicated Worker Threads or separate AWS Lambda functions; Implement Node.js Streams for memory-efficient processing.
contingency_plan: Vertical scaling of specific worker pods and implementing strict rate limiting on upload endpoints.
monitoring_trigger: Event Loop Lag > 100ms on Import Service pods via Prometheus/Grafana.

risk_description: AI Hallucination or extraction failure in SOW processing leading to inaccurate Project Briefs, resulting in poor vendor matching and manual rework.
root_cause: Variability in unstructured SOW document formats (PDF/Docx) and non-deterministic nature of LLMs.
mitigation_strategy: Mandatory Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) review step (US-034); Implement confidence scoring on extraction; Use few-shot prompting techniques to standardize outputs.
contingency_plan: Fallback to manual data entry mode if file parsing fails completely.
monitoring_trigger: High rate of 'Edit' actions on the SOW Review page indicating frequent AI inaccuracies.

risk_description: Vector search latency exceeding 250ms p95 requirement as the vendor/project dataset grows, degrading the recommendation engine user experience.
root_cause: Improper indexing configuration (e.g., missing IVFFlat or HNSW indexes) on PostgreSQL `pgvector` column.
mitigation_strategy: Implement HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) indexing for vector columns; Pre-warm database cache; Limit candidate pool based on hard filters (Status='Active') before vector comparison.
contingency_plan: Migrate vector search workload to a dedicated Pinecone or Milvus instance if RDS PostgreSQL creates bottlenecks.
monitoring_trigger: Database query latency for `/recommendations` endpoint exceeding 200ms.

risk_description: Incomplete PII redaction from SOW documents via AWS Comprehend before storage, leading to GDPR/privacy non-compliance.
root_cause: AWS Comprehend confidence thresholds set too high, missing specific entity types, or complex document layouts.
mitigation_strategy: Implement aggressive confidence thresholds (<0.8); Use regex overlays for specific patterns (emails, phones) alongside NLP; Store original only in highly restricted S3 bucket with strict lifecycle policies.
contingency_plan: Emergency script to re-process and scrub historical S3 objects if leakage is detected.
monitoring_trigger: Periodic manual audit of 'Sanitized' SOWs reveals unredacted PII.

risk_description: EKS Cluster cost overruns due to misconfigured Horizontal Pod Autoscaling (HPA) or unoptimized resource requests/limits for Node.js containers.
root_cause: Over-provisioning of resources or HPA triggers set too sensitively on CPU usage.
mitigation_strategy: Implement Kubecost for monitoring; Utilize AWS Spot Instances for stateless worker nodes; Fine-tune requests/limits based on load testing data (k6).
contingency_plan: Strict manual capacity pinning until autoscaling policies are tuned.
monitoring_trigger: AWS Budget Alert threshold breach (50% of monthly budget in week 1).

risk_description: Failure to meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards for complex interactive components (e.g., Data Tables, Drag-and-Drop Uploads), creating legal liability.
root_cause: Complexity of making dynamic Radix UI components fully screen-reader and keyboard accessible.
mitigation_strategy: Integrate `axe-core` into CI/CD pipeline (blocking builds on violation); Mandatory manual keyboard navigation testing for every UI PR.
contingency_plan: Engage external accessibility audit firm for remediation prior to public launch.
monitoring_trigger: CI pipeline failure rate on accessibility check steps.
